intro: Stenophragma humile is a plant species commonly known as "Humile" or "Wild Madder", belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Stenophragma humile include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stenophragma humile is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and infusions for various health applications.
